This stage provides the concrete local persistence layer for three kinds of durable state: cached plugins, encrypted secrets, and filesystem-backed memories. It sits in cross-cutting infrastructure rather than the main execution loop, supplying the on-disk layouts and safety rules that higher-level startup and runtime code rely on.

For plugins, core-plugins/src/store.rs owns the cache directory structure and the lifecycle for discovering installed versions, selecting the right one, installing new plugin contents, validating paths, and removing stale entries with mostly atomic filesystem updates. For secrets, secrets/src/lib.rs defines the public model—validated secret names, scopes, backend selection, and manager APIs—while secrets/src/local.rs implements the local encrypted file backend. On Windows, windows-sandbox-rs/src/dpapi.rs supplies machine-scoped DPAPI protection used to share protected secret material across privilege boundaries.

For memories, ext/memories/src/local.rs is the concrete backend and path gatekeeper. The write-side crate then materializes workspace files: memories/write/src/storage.rs rebuilds raw_memories.md and rollout summaries from database outputs, memories/write/src/control.rs safely clears workspace trees without following symlinks, and extensions/prune.rs removes expired extension resources. Together these pieces keep persistent local state structured, encrypted, and safely maintained.
